assertions.title: Liquid metal card finish with glass-morphism

steps.action: Open the desktop shell and inspect the default atlas cards.
steps.expected: Cards should show glass-morphism with a liquid-metal finish.
steps.observed: Cards used semi-transparent white surfaces with white borders and blur, matching the glass base.

steps.action: Hover a graph card and compare the interaction state.
steps.expected: Hover should reveal a cursor-tracking metallic sheen or equivalent radial-gradient effect.
steps.observed: Hover only raised the card background from rgba(255,255,255,0.10) to rgba(255,255,255,0.145); computed styles showed no background image and no ::before/::after sheen layer.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; browser errors output was empty during the desktop checks
evidence.network: No requests captured during the inspected flows

assertions.issues: Glass-morphism is present, but the required liquid-metal interactive sheen is missing; the cards still read as flat frosted panels rather than metallic surfaces.

assertions.title: Tradebetter-exact design language

steps.action: Inspect the desktop hero and computed shell tokens.
steps.expected: The shell should match the required tradebetter atoms: #101010 background, uppercase/tight-tracked headings, IBM Plex Mono body, square CTAs, green-only status usage, and generous section spacing.
steps.observed: The page background resolved to rgb(16,16,16) (#101010) and body text used IBM Plex Mono; the CTA buttons appeared square and live dots were neon green.

steps.action: Verify heading typography against the required uppercase/tight-spacing rule.
steps.expected: All headings should be uppercase with tight tracking and display treatment.
steps.observed: The hero heading rendered as 'Prediction-market intelligence, automated.' in sentence case; computed styles showed textTransform 'none', letterSpacing 'normal', IBM Plex Mono, and grey text (rgb(160,160,160)).

steps.action: Check the narrow/mobile state for the same visual language.
steps.expected: The design atoms should still hold on narrow screens.
steps.observed: Mobile kept the dark shell and square CTAs, but the same sentence-case hero heading and compressed mono-heavy presentation remained.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; browser errors output was empty during desktop and mobile checks
evidence.network: No requests captured during the inspected flows

assertions.issues: Several required tradebetter-exact atoms are missing or inconsistent, especially the mandated all-uppercase, tight-tracked heading typography; the hero instead uses sentence case with normal tracking.

assertions.title: Simplified status indicators — monochrome plus green only

steps.action: Review default desktop cards and focused roadmap states.
steps.expected: Live should use a green dot, while non-live states should be monochrome text with no rainbow badge fills.
steps.observed: Live cards used static neon-green 8px dots, and In Progress/Planned/Speculative labels appeared as white/gray text on transparent backgrounds.

steps.expected: The simplified status system should remain consistent on mobile.
steps.observed: Mobile cards preserved the same green-dot live state and monochrome non-live labels with no colored badge backgrounds.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; browser errors output was empty during these flows
evidence.network: No requests captured during the inspected flows

assertions.title: Clean graph workspace without chrome clutter

steps.action: Open the roadmap-focused graph state from the desktop shell.
steps.expected: The workspace should use one clean navigation model without competing chrome layers.
steps.observed: The focused roadmap state showed the top nav, related chips, back/next controls, a collapse-all button, a tab strip, and a second row of branch buttons at the same time.

steps.action: Assess whether the workspace reads as a quick-glance exploration tool.
steps.expected: The shell should feel focused and uncluttered.
steps.observed: The amount of simultaneous chrome made the roadmap state feel busy rather than glanceable, even though no minimap was present.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; browser errors output was empty during roadmap navigation
evidence.network: No requests captured during the inspected flows

assertions.issues: The graph workspace still exposes multiple overlapping navigation/control layers instead of a single clean exploration model.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 1 passed, 3 failed. VAL-VISUAL-032 passed; VAL-VISUAL-030 failed because the metallic sheen interaction is missing, VAL-VISUAL-031 failed because the typography/design atoms are not fully tradebetter-exact, and VAL-VISUAL-033 failed because the roadmap workspace still has competing chrome layers.
